requirement: Total number of grievances
reasoning: This requirement asks for a single summary count of grievances, which is best represented as a KPI card.

requirement: Total number of timely notifications of grievance decisions
reasoning: Timely notifications represent an ongoing compliance obligation with specific regulatory thresholds.

requirement: Number of expedited grievances
reasoning: This is a single summary count of grievances processed with an expedited priority.

requirement: Number of dismissed grievances
reasoning: This is a single summary count of grievances that resulted in a dismissal disposition.

requirement: Number of completed organization determinations (fully favorable, partially favorable, adverse)
reasoning: This requirement compares the counts of completed organization determinations across different disposition categories.

requirement: Number of completed reconsiderations (fully favorable, partially favorable, adverse)
reasoning: This requirement compares the counts of completed reconsiderations across different disposition categories.

requirement: Number of withdrawn coverage requests
reasoning: This is a single summary count of coverage requests that were withdrawn.

requirement: Number of dismissed coverage requests
reasoning: This is a single summary count of coverage requests that were dismissed.

requirement: Number of reopened organization determinations and reconsiderations
reasoning: This is a single summary count of reopened cases.

requirement: Employer Group Plan Sponsor enrollment count as of the last day of the reporting period
reasoning: This is a single summary count of active enrollees for a specific organization type at a point in time.

requirement: SNP Care Management: Number of eligible enrollees (Element A)
reasoning: This is a single summary count of eligible enrollees for SNP Care Management.

requirement: SNP Care Management: Number of initial HRAs completed (Element C)
reasoning: This is a single summary count of completed initial HRAs.

requirement: Payments to Providers: Total actual payments made to contracted providers (Element A)
reasoning: This is a single summary metric representing total actual payments.

requirement: Payments to Providers: Payments by value-based payment categories (Categories 1, 2, 3, and 4)
reasoning: This requirement compares payment amounts across different value-based payment categories.

requirement: Supplemental Benefit Utilization and Costs: Total net amount incurred by the plan (Element L)
reasoning: This is a single summary metric representing total net amount incurred.

requirement: D-SNP Transmission of Admission Notifications: Count of hospital and SNF admissions (Element A)
reasoning: This is a single summary count of admissions.

requirement: By Contract Number
reasoning: This dimension is used to compare metrics across different contract numbers.

requirement: By Plan Benefit Package (PBP) Category Code
reasoning: This dimension is used to compare metrics across different PBPs.

requirement: By State
reasoning: This dimension is used to compare metrics across different states.

requirement: In-network payments only (for Payments to Providers Elements B-S)
reasoning: This filter rule ensures only in-network payments are included, acting as a data validation/quality check.

requirement: Beneficiary-initiated enrollment and disenrollment requests (excluding auto-assignments and CMS-initiated actions)
reasoning: This filter rule excludes auto-assignments and CMS-initiated actions, ensuring data quality.

requirement: Grievances are reported based on the date the MAO provided the decision, regardless of when the request was received.
reasoning: This defines the compliance reporting window and logic based on decision date.

requirement: Reopened cases must be reported in the quarter's file corresponding to when the beneficiary was notified of the reopening decision.
reasoning: This is an ongoing compliance obligation defining when reopened cases must be reported.

requirement: For Payments to Providers, if a provider is paid using multiple arrangements, report total dollars under the category representing the dominant payment arrangement.
reasoning: This is a data quality rule defining how to handle and allocate provider payments across multiple arrangements.

requirement: SNP Care Management: To report an enrollee as 'unable to reach' (UTR), the plan must document at least 3 non-automated phone attempts and a follow-up letter.
reasoning: This is a compliance monitoring rule defining the documentation threshold for UTR status.

requirement: Complaints Tracking Module (CTM) records
reasoning: This is an exclusion rule to filter out CTM records to ensure data quality.

requirement: Grievances filed by non-enrollees or prospective enrollees
reasoning: This is an exclusion rule to filter out non-enrollees to ensure data quality.

requirement: Withdrawn grievances
reasoning: This is an exclusion rule to filter out withdrawn grievances to ensure data quality.

requirement: Dismissed grievances (excluded from the total grievance count, reported separately)
reasoning: This is an exclusion rule to filter out dismissed grievances from the total count to ensure data quality.

requirement: Decision Rationale descriptive reasons only (no numerical prefixes)
reasoning: This is a data quality rule ensuring that numerical prefixes are removed from the decision rationale field per organizational decisions 4fac028f and 95cb9416.

requirement: Ensure that the Medicare Part C Reporting is kept strictly separate from prior authorization metrics and service level data collection reports.
reasoning: This is a compliance monitoring rule ensuring strict isolation of Part C reporting from prior authorization metrics per organizational decision c19cd11c.
